question: One full Earth rotation takes this many hours?
fun_fact: Earth's rotation is actually slowing down by about 1.7 milliseconds every century.
joke: If Earth spun any slower, Mondays would last forever!

question: Shape with eight sides is called what?
fun_fact: An octagon has eight sides.
joke: Most octagons have more sides than squares—unless you ask a confused mathematician!

question: Earth’s largest desert is actually found in...?
fun_fact: Antarctica is technically a cold desert because it receives very little precipitation.
joke: It's the only desert where you need a parka, not a parasol!

question: Which insect has colorful wings and drinks nectar?
fun_fact: Butterflies taste with their feet.
joke: Imagine if you could taste pizza just by stepping on it!

question: Who wrote the play “Romeo and Juliet”?
fun_fact: Shakespeare is credited with inventing over 1,700 words in the English language.
joke: He probably invented the 'selfie' too, but forgot to write it down!

question: What is the main ingredient in guacamole?
fun_fact: Avocados are technically berries, not vegetables.
joke: So guacamole is basically a fruit salad!

question: Which planet has the most moons?
fun_fact: Saturn has over 80 confirmed moons, more than any other planet in our solar system.
joke: Saturn's moon parties are out of this world!

question: What is the fastest land animal?
fun_fact: Cheetahs can run up to 75 mph (120 km/h).
joke: If a cheetah asks for a ride, just say no—your car can't keep up!

question: Which country invented paper?
fun_fact: Paper was invented in China around 105 AD by Cai Lun.
joke: Before paper, people just had really long memories!

question: What is the smallest bone in the body?
fun_fact: The stapes, located in the middle ear, is the smallest bone in the human body.
joke: It's so small, even a mouse would need glasses to see it!

question: Who was the first person on the Moon?
fun_fact: Neil Armstrong's footprint is still on the Moon because there's no wind to erase it.
joke: He forgot to wipe his boots before coming back!

question: What is the only continent with no reptiles?
fun_fact: Antarctica is the only continent with no native reptiles due to its extreme cold.
joke: Even snakes need a scarf down there!

question: What is gold’s chemical symbol?
fun_fact: The symbol 'Au' for gold comes from the Latin word 'aurum'.
joke: Gold's symbol is 'Au'—as in, 'Au, that's expensive!'

question: In which year did World War Two end?
fun_fact: World War Two ended in 1945 when Japan surrendered aboard the USS Missouri.
joke: The world finally got a break from history class!

question: Smallest prime number above 100?
fun_fact: A prime number is only divisible by itself and one.
joke: 101 is so prime, it refuses to be divided—even by peer pressure!

question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
fun_fact: Mars appears red because its surface is covered in iron oxide, also known as rust.
joke: Mars: the only planet that looks like it's constantly blushing!

question: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
fun_fact: The Pacific Ocean is so vast it could hold all of Earth's continents with room to spare.
joke: If you tried to drink the Pacific, you'd need a really big straw!

question: How many bones in the adult human body?
fun_fact: Babies are born with about 300 bones, but adults have 206 as some fuse together.
joke: That's a lot of bones to keep track of at playtime!

question: Who painted the Sistine Chapel ceiling?
fun_fact: Michelangelo was primarily a sculptor, but painted the Sistine Chapel ceiling.
joke: He got a real pain in the neck from that job!

question: What is Earth’s hardest natural substance?
fun_fact: Diamonds are made of pure carbon compressed under extreme pressure.
joke: Diamonds: proof that even carbon can shine under pressure!

question: Who gave the Statue of Liberty to USA?
fun_fact: France gave the Statue of Liberty to the USA in 1886 as a symbol of friendship.
joke: Some assembly required—thanks, France!

question: What is the capital city of Japan?
fun_fact: Tokyo was once a small fishing village called Edo.
joke: From sushi to skyscrapers—Tokyo's glow-up is real!

question: Which gas do plants absorb during photosynthesis?
fun_fact: Plants absorb carbon dioxide (CO2) during photosynthesis and release oxygen.
joke: Plants: Earth's original air fresheners!

question: Which British battle was fought in 1066?
fun_fact: The Battle of Hastings in 1066 led to the Norman Conquest of England.
joke: The Normans really put the 'conquer' in 'conquest'!

question: What is the highest mountain in the world?
fun_fact: Mount Everest is the highest mountain on Earth, at 8,848 meters (29,029 ft).
joke: Climbing Everest: the ultimate uphill battle!

question: What year did the Titanic sink?
fun_fact: The Titanic sank in 1912 after hitting an iceberg on its maiden voyage.
joke: Even the unsinkable can have a bad day!

question: The moon landing: What year was it?
fun_fact: Apollo 11 landed on the Moon on July 20, 1969. Neil Armstrong was the first to step out.
joke: One small step for man, one giant leap for moon dust!

question: What year did the Berlin Wall fall?
fun_fact: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, leading to German reunification.
joke: The wall came down faster than a bad soufflé!

question: What year did Columbus first reach the Americas?
fun_fact: Columbus reached the Americas in 1492, landing in the Bahamas.
joke: He was looking for India, but found a whole new world instead!
